steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure of the clause
steps.content:
The main noun phrase is "a digital resource that, _____ text and music clips, traces the history." The relative clause introduced by that already has a finite main verb: traces. The blank sits between two commas, which signals a non-finite participial phrase modifying how the resource traces history — not a second finite verb.
  • Choice A — integrates: Simple present finite verb — placing two finite verbs (integrates and traces) in one clause without a conjunction creates a run-on. ✗
  • Choice B — is integrating: Present progressive finite verb — same run-on problem as A; two finite verbs in the clause with no coordinator. ✗
  • Choice C — integrating: Present participle — non-finite form that can modify the clause without competing with traces, correctly forming a participial phrase. ✓
  • Choice D — has integrated: Present perfect finite verb — same run-on problem as A and B. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only a present participle can appear as an adverbial modifier between commas in a clause that already has a finite verb. That matches Choice C — integrating.
